app.name: QuiverKit
app.tagline: Developer tools that never leave your browser

home.heading: Every tool you keep googling for
home.subheading: Base64, JWT, hashes, UUIDs and more. Everything runs on your machine — no upload, no account, no tracking.

privacy.note: Nothing you type here is sent anywhere.

error.emptyInput: Type or paste something to get started.
error.invalidUtf8: Decoded successfully, but the result is binary, not text.
error.invalidJwt: This is not a valid JWT. A token has three dot-separated parts.
error.invalidTimestamp: This is not a date or a Unix timestamp.

base64.name: Base64
base64.description: Encode and decode Base64, with URL-safe output and full Unicode support.

jwt.name: JWT Decoder
jwt.description: Inspect a JSON Web Token's header, payload and expiry — without sending it anywhere.
jwt.signatureNote: The signature is shown but not verified: checking it needs the signing key, and a tool that asks for your key is a tool you should not use.

url.name: URL Encoder
url.description: Percent-encode and decode URLs, or split one into its parts.
url.componentHint: Escapes everything unsafe, including / ? & = — use this for a single value.
url.wholeHint: Leaves reserved characters intact — use this for a complete address.
url.parseHint: Splits an address into protocol, host, path and query parameters.

json.name: JSON Formatter
json.description: Format, minify and alphabetically sort JSON.

timestamp.name: Timestamp Converter
timestamp.description: Convert between Unix time, ISO 8601 and human-readable dates.
timestamp.hint: Accepts Unix seconds, Unix milliseconds or any date string.

hash.name: Hash Generator
hash.description: SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-384 and SHA-512 digests, with optional HMAC.
hash.secretPlaceholder: Leave empty for a plain hash
hash.secretHint: Fill this in to sign the input with HMAC instead of hashing it.
hash.md5Note: MD5 is missing on purpose. Browsers refuse to implement it because it is broken, and adding our own would only encourage its use.

uuid.name: UUID Generator
uuid.description: Generate UUID v4, time-ordered UUID v7 and Nano IDs.
uuid.v7Hint: Time-ordered, so it sorts by creation. Better as a database key.
